text.title: Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: The checklist MUST run weekly. Bi-weekly = drift; monthly = the rot returns in full.
rule.rationale: Habit-formation research — anchors Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist rule `weekly-cadence`.

rule.statement: Items idle >90 days with zero new comment/activity MUST be closed or explicitly snoozed with a revisit date. No silent backlog growth.
rule.rationale: Backlog-rot research — anchors Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist rule `stale-sweep-90d`.

rule.statement: Duplicates MUST be merged (winner inherits comments + votes), not just labeled. Living duplicates poison search.
rule.rationale: Tracker-hygiene practice — anchors Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist rule `dup-merge-mandatory`.

rule.statement: The top 10 MUST be published in the team's primary channel each week. Private top-10 = no alignment.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder-visibility research — anchors Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist rule `top-10-published`.

rule.statement: Anti-roadmap MUST be re-touched (review expirations, add new declines). Stale anti-roadmap = repeat 'what about X' questions.
rule.rationale: Anti-decay practice — anchors Backlog Hygiene Cron Checklist rule `anti-roadmap-refresh`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
